claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for:**

How old is your child? Please let me know which category applies:

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, please let me know each of their names and ages, and we can assess eligibility for each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim for my son Alex - he's 6 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely to meet you! Thanks for that information about Alex. Since he's 6 years old, he falls into the **under 16** category, which is great news for eligibility purposes.

Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is only 6 years old, he wouldn't be in paid employment, so this check is straightforward.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

I expect the answer is **yes** given his age - can you confirm he's not in regular paid employment?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - he's only 6, so he's definitely not working at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

This refers to benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that are claimed by the child themselves. Given Alex is only 6, he almost certainly wouldn't be receiving these benefits in his own name.

Can you confirm Alex isn't receiving any such benefits in his own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex in an apprenticeship?**

Given that Alex is only 6 years old, I'm confident he wouldn't be in any kind of apprenticeship. Can you confirm that Alex is **not** in an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's just 6, so he's in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, Alex being in primary school makes sense! Now we need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and meet one of these conditions:
   - You're working and earning above the National Insurance threshold
   - You're actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
   - You have sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain benefits like Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
   - You're a student with sufficient financial resources
   - You're a family member of an EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
4. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Which of these applies to your situation? Or simply let me know if you normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I don't actually live in the UK - I live abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you live abroad, I need to ask a follow-up question:

**Are you a Crown servant living abroad?**

Crown servants include people who work for the UK government overseas, such as:
- Members of the Armed Forces stationed abroad
- Diplomats and embassy staff
- Civil servants posted overseas

Alternatively, are you living in certain qualifying countries with special arrangements with the UK?

If none of these apply, you may unfortunately not meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit. Could you tell me more about your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant - I'm just living abroad as a regular person.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to share this news, but based on the information you've provided, I've reached a conclusion about your eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Outcome: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for UK Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Why?

While Alex himself meets all the criteria for being a qualifying child (he's under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements**.

To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must:
- Normally live in the UK, **or**
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions), **or**
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

Since you live abroad as a regular person and don't fall into any of these categories, you unfortunately cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

### What can you do?

- **If your circumstances change** and you move back to the UK, you would then be able to apply for Child Benefit for Alex.
- **Check if the country you live in** has its own child benefit or family allowance scheme that you might be eligible for.
- If you believe there may be a special arrangement between the UK and the country where you reside, you could contact HMRC directly to discuss your specific situation.

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and meets all the child-related eligibility criteria: he is under 16, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad and is not a Crown servant or in any other qualifying category (such as having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme). UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific residency exceptions, which are not met in this case.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is seeking Child Benefit for their 6-year-old son Alex. While Alex himself qualifies as an eligible child, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they live abroad and are not a Crown servant or in any other qualifying residency category. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific residency exceptions, which are not met in this case.
